title: Spuriopimpinella calycina
taxonomy: Spuriopimpinella calycina is classified within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It belongs to the genus Pimpinella. This plant is further grouped within the subfamily Apioideae. Pimpinella species are often referred to as bishop's weed. Spuriopimpinella calycina is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia.
uses: Spuriopimpinella calycina has been studied for its potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. Its plant parts, including the leaves and stems, have been used to treat various health issues. Research suggests that the plant may have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. Further research is required to fully explore the medicinal potential of Spuriopimpinella calycina.
